page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-64-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4a server family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include any GPU accelerators, and the local storage size is specified as None. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this configuration.

Due to the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density, this instance class is designed for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity per core without the overhead of shared-core resource contention. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a dense memory configuration relative to compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive deployments that do not require local physical storage or GPU acceleration.

This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and data analytics pipelines that process large datasets in system memory.

It also provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for memory-intensive workloads, this instance offers high resource density for applications requiring substantial memory capacity without local storage dependencies. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data analytics pipelines that operate entirely within system memory.

tagline: An x86_64 server configuration featuring sixty-four dedicated virtual cores and a high-density five-hundred-and-twelve gigabyte memory array.
bullet_points: 512.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Zero GPU accelerators and no local storage
Suited for in-memory databases and caching layers
